docs.title: About this page
docs.text: The AusTrakka NAT provides expert analysis and interpretation of genomic and epidemiological data to support public health outcomes in Australia. This page provides details of bioinformatics analysis approaches.

docs.title: Quality control
docs.text:
All sequences submitted to AusTrakka undergo quality assessment to determine the suitability of the sequences for further analysis.

QC metric Criteria Bioinformatics tool Species observed in sequence data Most abundant species Salmonella enterica kraken2 with latest pluspf database Serotype detected Enteritidis sistr Estimated genome size (reads) 4.7 Mbp +/- 10% kmc Assembled genome size 4.7 Mbp +/- 10% seqkit stats Estimated average depth of coverage 40x number reads / esitmated genome size (reads)

Sequences which pass these criteria are used in downstream analsyis.

docs.title: Single sample results
docs.text: MLST and detection of AMR mechanisms is also undertaken on all sequences which pass the quality assessment.

docs.title: Core genome MLST
docs.text:
Core genome MLST is used to group sequences into cgMLST groups for further detailed analysis. This approach allows for maximisation of core genome recovery and high resolution interpretations of relatedness to aid in informing the degree of relatedness.

cgMLST as implemented by the ANAT, utilises a scheme of 3016 genes and the allele calling tool chewBBACA v2.6.0 to detect profiles.

Only sequences which have >90% alleles detected are used in further analysis.

Distances between profiles is calculated with cgmlst-dists (v1.2.0) and these distances are clustered using heirarchical complete linkage with a 50 allele threshold. This method identifies groups where all sequences within the group are <= 50 alleles from every other sequence in the group.
